ORDER OVERRULING DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RAISKIN & REVITZ AND STEVEN REVITZ'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. OVERRULED. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY PLEADS PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE AND BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY. THE COMPLAINT PROPERLY ALLEGES WHY PLAINTIFFS COULD NOT HAVE AVOIDED THE DAMAGES CLAIMED. CORPORATIONS CODE 419 DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE THE SHARES WERE NOT LOST, STOLEN OR DESTROYED, BUT RATHER HAD APPARENTLY BEEN ERRONEOUSLY DELIVERED TO THE BUYERS BY DEFENDANTS. FURTHER, THE COMPLAINT ARTICULATES WHY PLAINTIFFS DID NOT SUE FOR RETURN OF THE SHARES FROM THE BUYERS. SUPPOSED REDUNDANCY OF CAUSES OF ACTION IS NOT A BASIS FOR DEMURRER. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice
Off Calendar May-24-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT RAISKIN & REVITZ, STEVEN REVITZ's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendants failed to satisfy their initial burden of showing that the causes of action for legal malpractice and breach of fiduciary duties alleged in plaintiffs Keith and Inice Beverly's second amended complaint are without merit. Defendants have not negated plaintiffs' theory of liability alleged in the SAC that, as a result of defendants' conduct, plaintiffs were unable "to exercise their rights, including those of ownership and liquidation, to recoup the outstanding remaining obligations of the buyers." (SAC 28.) Instead, defendants moved for summary judgment on an issue - that the missing share certificates did not affect the terms of the settlement between plaintiffs and the buyers - which is not alleged in the SAC. Thus, defendants' motion must be denied because the actual allegations in the SAC frame the issues for a summary motion, not defendants' recasting of those allegations. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Rogers Joseph O'Donnell, Esq. (415-365-5325) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, James D. Hepworth of Nemecek & Cole (818-7889500) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS RAISKIN & REVITZ and STEVEN REVITZ'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. The second amended complaint adequately pleads professional negligence and breach of fiduciary duty. The complaint properly alleges why plaintiffs could not have avoided the damages claimed. Corporations Code 419 does not apply because the shares were not lost, stolen or destroyed, but rather had apparently been erroneously delivered to the buyers by defendants. Further, the complaint articulates why plaintiffs did not sue for return of the shares from the buyers. Supposed redundancy of causes of action is not a basis for demurrer. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

Defendants Raiskin & Revitz And Steven Revitz'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Starre of Nemecek & Cole telephone number 818-788-9500 appeared by telephone for Defendants Raiskin & Revitz and Steven J. Revitz.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants Raiskin & Revitz And Steven Revitz's Demurrer To both causes of action in the first amended complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. It cannot be determined from the face of the first amended complaint whether defendants could have avoided the damages they claim to have suffered by making efforts to obtain the pledged share certificates. The claim for breach of fiduciary duty is wholly duplicative of the claim for professional negligence. To state a claim for breach of fiduciary duty separate from a claim for professional negligence, plaintiffs must allege a breach of some fiduciary duty other than the duty to provide competent legal services. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege facts: 1) explaining that the damages they suffered could not have been avoided by their making efforts to obtain the pledged share certificates and 2) supporting a claim for breach of fiduciary duty based on something other than negligent conduct by the defendants. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/HK)
